summary: oh-langfuse setup CLI silently relays AI coding session transcripts to a hardcoded HTTP endpoint using bundled Langfuse keys
details: When a user runs the documented `npx oh-langfuse setup <claude|opencode|codex>` flow, the package configures Claude Code, opencode, and Codex to send their full session transcripts to a hardcoded destination controlled by the package author. bin/cli.js lines 10-12 define `DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_BASE_URL = "http://120.46.221.227:3000"` along with bundled `pk-lf-da0c90a7-...` / `sk-lf-0269b85d-...` Langfuse credentials, and the setup scripts (scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs, scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs) write these defaults into `~/.claude/settings.json`, `~/.config/opencode/opencode-plugin-langfuse/config.json`, and `~/.codex/langfuse/config.json`. The setup additionally edits `~/.bashrc`/`~/.zshrc` and the Windows user environment to export `LANGFUSE_BASEURL=http://120.46.221.227:3000` together with the bundled keys, and prepends a wrapper shim directory to PATH so future invocations of `claude`/`opencode`/`codex` keep re-applying these values. The installed Stop/notify hooks then read each session's transcript and POST it over plain HTTP to 120.46.221.227:3000 (a Huawei Cloud Beijing address) under the bundled project keys. Net effect: any developer who follows the README delivers their AI-coding sessions — which routinely contain pasted source, tokens, and secrets — to an author-controlled server in cleartext. The destination is shown briefly during setup but defaults are accepted by most users. Two secondary concerns also surface: a fallback path in scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs would, if the bundled `langfuse_hook.py` is missing, download `https://gitcode.com/user-attachments/files/8187690/7a797a5314b9497cae7b055aa51be646.zip` (an unauthenticated, unverified attachment) and install its contents as Stop hooks; and the shell-rc / Windows-env modifications make the redirection persistent across future CLI invocations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
